# Problem Definition

Higher education students lack personalized learning pathways that adapt to their individual knowledge gaps and learning styles. Current LMS platforms deliver uniform content regardless of student readiness, leading to 40% dropout rates in STEM courses.

## Core Problem

The disconnect between how students learn and how content is delivered creates a compounding knowledge gap that existing educational technology fails to address.

## Target Population

- Undergraduate STEM students (US universities)
- First-generation college students (highest dropout risk)
- Non-traditional learners (working adults, part-time students)

## Desired Outcome

Reduce STEM course dropout rates by 25% through AI-driven personalized learning pathways that adapt in real-time to student performance signals.
